channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: White Wolf Announces Vampire: The Masquerade – Bound by Blood Coming to D&D Beyond
item.content:encoded:
Ghostfire Games has announced new partnered content is coming to D&D Beyond. The twist is the book is Vampire: The Masquerade – Bound by Blood, a collaboration with White Wolf designed to bring a bit of World of Darkness into Dungeons & Dragons.

In a recent post on D&D Beyond, Wizards of the Coast announced several third party books that would be featured on the digital platform under Partnered Content. Some of these books include popular crowdfunding successes like Steinhardt's Guide to the Eldritch Hunt by MonkeyDM, Hit Point Press' The Field Guide to Floral Dragons.

But among those announcements are Vampire: The Masquerade – Bound by Blood. According to the blurb, the book will contain an official Kindred class for Dungeons & Dragons, one that utilizes Blood Points and Disciplines while struggling to control the Beast within. The book will also contain a prewritten adventure set within the Dark Ages. 

In other words, the book will be adapting the bloodsuckers from Vampire: The Masquerade into the Dungeons & Dragons system set in a medieval era to fit within the game's fantasy trappings. Notably, this is the first piece of recent Vampire: The Masquerade material made under the returning White Wolf brand.

Vampire: The Masquerade – Bound by Blood is being designed by Ghostfire Games. This is a studio known for horror-adjacent dark fantasy material. Their work includes the bleak Grim Hollow books, developing Dungeons of Drakkenheim with the Dungeon Dudes, and the space opera-themed Aetherial Expanse. Given their experience with settings full of monsters, devilish deals, and unsettling monsters, they're a good fit for World of Darkness.

The reasons for this book happening are interesting. On paper, Vampire: The Masquerade and Dungeons & Dragons are different beasts. V:tM was a pioneer in roleplay-heavy experiences. A game more focused on intrigue, political alliances, and social climbing with the occasional outburst of violence. D&D is a mechanically complex pulp adventure game with the forces of good and evil duking it out. Seeing how one is adapted to the other has a certain novelty to it.

In addition, there is the matter of getting more players interested in World of Darkness. Having material on D&D Beyond may get more people aware of Vampire: The Masquerade. This is especially important since White Wolf announced a new V:tM project will be debuting at this year's Gen Con. In that context, Bound by Blood may be seen as a stepping on point for a new generation of players.

Preorders for Vampire: The Masquerade – Bound by Blood are available now. The material will go live on D&D Beyond in July.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler
